proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_001 (ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with feature_014=1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_014=0.
Patients with feature_027=1 have longer pfs_months than feature_027=0 (candidate treatment).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.linregress(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive linear association: slope=+0.176 months/unit, Pearson r=0.766, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.linregress(df['feature_001'], df['pfs_months']); group means by level
analyses.result_summary: Strong negative ordinal association: slope=-1.190/level, p<1e-300; mean PFS by level: 0=5.27, 1=4.05, 2=2.91 months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_014']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_014']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1: mean PFS 3.70 mo vs feature_014=0: 5.06 mo; diff -1.35 mo; p<1e-300.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_027']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_027']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_027=1: mean PFS 5.09 mo vs feature_027=0: 4.12 mo; diff +0.97 mo; p=1.7e-216.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining continuous biochemistry features (feature_005, feature_019, feature_021, feature_025, feature_032, feature_017, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009), at least one shows a non-trivial main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each continuous feature: scipy.stats.linregress(feat, pfs)
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 slope=+0.465/unit (r=0.10, p=3e-115); feature_025 slope=-0.073/unit (r=-0.12, p=2e-167); feature_005 slope=-0.005/unit (r=-0.04, p=3e-17). Other 14 continuous features have |r|<=0.013 — minor or no main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining binary features, additional ones show a significant main effect on pfs_months beyond feature_014 and feature_027.

analyses.code: for each binary feature c: ttest_ind(pfs|c=1, pfs|c=0)
analyses.result_summary: Significant: feature_006 (-0.31 mo, p=1e-49), feature_023 (-0.33 mo, p=2e-58), feature_011 (+0.22 mo, p=7e-4), feature_022 (-0.23 mo, p=3e-7). Non-significant (p>0.05): feature_031, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_016, feature_028, feature_002.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is independent of all other features in the dataset (consistent with a randomly assigned treatment), so its association with pfs_months is causal rather than confounded.

analyses.code: for each other feature c: chi-square (binary) or Welch t-test (continuous) of c by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Every comparison non-significant (all p>0.10): feature_015 p=0.16, feature_001 p=0.12, feature_014 p=0.33, feature_006 p=0.34, feature_005 p=0.86, feature_019 p=0.14, etc. feature_027 appears completely balanced across all 32 other features — strongly consistent with randomized treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on all 33 features simultaneously, feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_027, feature_023, feature_006, feature_019, feature_025, feature_005, feature_022 retain independent signed effects in the directions seen univariately, while most other features become non-significant.

analyses.code: statsmodels.api.OLS(pfs, sm.add_constant(df[features_all])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.859. Significant adjusted coefficients (sorted by |t|): feature_015 +0.176 (t=454), feature_001 -1.171 (t=-206), feature_014 -1.362 (t=-176), feature_027 +0.943 (t=98), feature_025 -0.076 (t=-76), feature_019 +0.469 (t=61), feature_023 -0.354 (t=-44), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-36), feature_005 -0.005 (t=-21), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-19), feature_021 -0.0004 (t=-9). All other 22 features have p>0.05 in the adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is heterogeneous and modified by other features; in particular, at least one other feature interacts significantly with feature_027 in a model pfs ~ trt + feat + trt:feat.

analyses.code: for each candidate modifier feat: OLS(pfs ~ trt + feat + trt:feat); record p of trt:feat
analyses.result_summary: Strong interactions (sorted by interaction p-value): trt:feature_023 p=8e-226, coef=-1.63 (effect abolished if feature_023=1); trt:feature_006 p=4e-168, coef=-1.45 (effect abolished if feature_006=1); trt:feature_005 p=9e-58, coef=-0.025 per unit (effect declines as feature_005 increases); trt:feature_022 p=6e-23, coef=-1.18; trt:feature_011 p=1e-11, coef=+0.99 (effect amplified if feature_011=1). All other interactions p>0.10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect: when feature_023=0 the treatment effect is large and positive, but when feature_023=1 there is no treatment effect.
feature_006 abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect: when feature_006=0 the treatment effect is large and positive, but when feature_006=1 there is no treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratify by (feature_023, feature_006); ttest treatment effect within each cell

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 also abolishes the feature_027 treatment effect, independently of feature_023 and feature_006.
feature_011=1 amplifies the feature_027 treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_022; ttest treatment effect within each level
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0: n_trt=9554, diff=+1.027 mo, p=5e-226. feature_022=1: n_trt=468, diff=-0.156 mo, p=0.13 (n.s., trend toward harm).

analyses.code: stratify by feature_011; ttest treatment effect within each level
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0: n_trt=9709, diff=+0.941 mo, p=2e-199. feature_011=1: n_trt=313, diff=+1.929 mo, p=3e-21. Apparent doubling.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 receive a much larger treatment effect from feature_027 than patients outside this triple-negative subgroup, in whom the effect is null.

analyses.code: subgroup mask m = (023==0)&(006==0)&(022==0); ttest treatment effect inside m and outside m
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=17372): n_trt=3461, n_ctrl=13911, mean_trt=6.875 mo, mean_ctrl=4.117 mo, diff=+2.758 mo, p<1e-300. Outside subgroup (n=32628): n_trt=6561, diff=+0.030 mo, p=0.29 (no detectable effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0) subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_027 declines as feature_005 increases — feature_005 is an additional, continuous-scale modifier.

analyses.code: within triple-negative subgroup: OLS(pfs ~ trt + feature_005 + trt:feature_005); also OLS with log1p(feature_005)
analyses.result_summary: Linear-scale interaction: trt coef=+3.35, trt:feature_005 coef=-0.065 per unit, p=7e-136. Log-scale: trt:log(1+feature_005) coef=-2.18 per log unit, p<1e-300. Strong, monotone attenuation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative subgroup, treatment effect is large for patients with low feature_005 (e.g. <=5) and absent for patients with high feature_005 (>5).

analyses.code: stratify triple-negative subgroup by feature_005 quartiles and at cutoffs 1..10
analyses.result_summary: Quartiles of feature_005 within triple-negative subgroup: q0 (0.04-1.97) diff=+5.03; q1 (1.98-4.47) diff=+5.03; q2 (4.48-9.90) diff=+0.66; q3 (9.91-442) diff=-0.02. Cutoff scan: at cut=5, low diff=+5.014, high diff=-0.002. Cutoff ~5 (clinical CEA upper limit of normal) cleanly separates responders from non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5 derive a treatment effect of feature_027 of approximately +5 months PFS, whereas patients failing any one of these four conditions derive essentially no benefit.

analyses.code: ttest treatment effect inside the four-feature subgroup (n=9338) and bootstrap (500 reps)
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup: n_trt=1905, n_ctrl=7433, mean_trt=9.103 mo, mean_ctrl=4.089 mo, diff=+5.014 mo, p<1e-300; bootstrap 95% CI [4.91, 5.12].

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four predicates (feature_023=0, feature_006=0, feature_022=0, feature_005<=5) are independently necessary: violating any one of them, while keeping the other three, abolishes the treatment effect.

analyses.code: for each predicate i: subset where predicate i is violated but the other three hold; ttest treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Violate 023=0 only: n_trt=1482, diff=+0.068, p=0.26 (n.s.). Violate 006=0 only: n_trt=1022, diff=+0.113, p=0.12 (n.s.). Violate 022=0 only: n_trt=155, diff=-0.165, p=0.38 (n.s.). Violate f5<=5 only: n_trt=1556, diff=-0.002, p=0.97 (n.s.). Each predicate alone is sufficient to eliminate the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint interaction model with all four modifiers entered together, each trt:modifier coefficient remains highly significant, confirming the four predicates are independently required.

analyses.result_summary: trt:feature_023 coef=-1.78, p=2e-227; trt:feature_006 coef=-1.46, p=4e-170; trt:feature_022 coef=-1.90, p=3e-58; trt:I(feature_005<=5) coef=+1.74, p=2e-260. Baseline trt coef (all modifiers unfavourable) = +1.38; with all four favourable predicates, total trt effect = 1.38 + 1.74 = +3.12 (this is the all-modifiers-zero reference plus the f5<=5 boost; group means above show the actual stratum mean is +5.01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all 16 cells of the four-modifier 2x2x2x2 partition are tabulated, only one cell — the all-favourable cell — shows a significant, large treatment effect; the other 15 cells are null.

analyses.code: for each of 16 cells in (023, 006, 022, I(feature_005<=5)): ttest treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Only cell (023=0, 006=0, 022=0, feature_005<=5) has diff=+5.014 (p<1e-300). All other 15 cells have |diff|<0.25 mo and p>=0.12 (none reach the 0.05 threshold even uncorrected). This is a knife-edge subgroup pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive search over pairs and triples of the binary features (each set to 0) does not uncover a binary-only subgroup with a larger treatment effect than the (feature_023=0, feature_006=0, feature_022=0) triple.

analyses.code: for c1<c2 in binaries: subgroup df[c1==0 & c2==0]; ttest. Same for triples.
analyses.result_summary: Top binary pair: feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 diff=+2.520. Top triple: feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_022=0 diff=+2.758 (best triple). Adding any other binary feature only modestly increases the diff at the cost of dropping sample size and tracks the (006=0 & 023=0 & 022=0) subgroup. No purely binary combination beats the triple.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature behaves like a treatment (i.e. yields a substantial subgroup-conditional pfs effect when conditioned on a single best partner feature).

analyses.code: for cand in [feature_024, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020]: find best partner f giving max |E[pfs|cand=1, f=0] - E[pfs|cand=0, f=0]|
analyses.result_summary: Best subgroup-conditional effects: feature_024 +0.06 mo (p=0.08); feature_010 +0.03 mo (p=0.44); feature_012 -0.07 mo (p=0.16); feature_018 +0.04 mo (p=0.44); feature_020 -0.08 mo (p=0.14). None reach magnitude or significance comparable to feature_027. feature_027 is the only treatment-like exposure in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the four-condition responder subgroup, the treatment effect is homogeneous across feature_001 (performance-status-like ordinal), feature_014 (binary), and feature_011 (rare biomarker) — these prognostic factors do not further stratify the treatment effect.

analyses.code: within responder subgroup: stratify by feature_001 (0/1/2), feature_014 (0/1), feature_011 (0/1), feature_015 (continuous); ttest or interaction OLS
analyses.result_summary: feature_001: diffs by level 0/1/2 = +4.87/+5.09/+4.81 (all p<1e-100). feature_014: 0/1 = +5.03/+5.01. feature_011: 0/1 = +5.00/+5.24. feature_015 interaction p=0.11 (not significant). Effect is uniformly ~+5 mo across these strata. Treatment benefit does not depend on prognostic state once the four-modifier subgroup is fixed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The interaction between feature_027 and feature_011 seen in the marginal screen (h13) is explained by feature_011's mild positive prognostic effect plus correlation with the responder subgroup membership; in a model containing feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_005 interactions, the trt:feature_011 interaction is no longer significant.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ trt + each main + each trt:main) for mods=[023,006,022,011,005]
analyses.result_summary: In the full multivariable interaction model, trt:feature_011 coef=+0.16, p=0.27 (not significant), while trt:feature_023, trt:feature_006, trt:feature_022, trt:feature_005 remain p<<0.001. The apparent feature_011 amplification is explained away once the four core modifiers are conditioned on.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative subgroup (feature_023=0, feature_006=0, feature_022=0), the treatment effect of feature_027 is approximately linear in log(feature_005), declining by ~1.66 months per log-unit of feature_005.

analyses.code: within triple-negative subgroup: OLS(pfs ~ trt + log(feature_005) + trt:log(feature_005))
analyses.result_summary: trt main coef=+5.17 (at log(feature_005)=0, i.e. feature_005=1); trt:log(feature_005) coef=-1.66 per log unit, p<1e-300. Strong, smooth attenuation rather than abrupt threshold; the cut at feature_005=5 is operationally close to the point where the linear predictor crosses zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (likely performance status) shows a graded prognostic effect on pfs_months across its three levels (0 > 1 > 2), with each pairwise contrast highly significant.

analyses.code: pairwise Welch t-tests across feature_001 levels {0,1,2}
analyses.result_summary: 0 vs 1: 5.27 vs 4.05 mo, diff=+1.22, p<1e-300. 0 vs 2: 5.27 vs 2.91, diff=+2.36, p<1e-300. 1 vs 2: 4.05 vs 2.91, diff=+1.15, p<1e-300. Monotone graded prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031 (binary, ~45% prevalence — likely sex) has no detectable association with pfs_months either marginally or after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(pfs|feature_031=1, pfs|feature_031=0); also coefficient in multivariable OLS
analyses.result_summary: Marginal diff = -0.006 mo, p=0.77. Adjusted coefficient +0.010, p=0.19. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the rare binary biomarkers (prevalence 0.5%-5%: feature_002, feature_011, feature_016, feature_022, feature_028), only feature_022 (resistance modifier) and feature_011 (apparent amplifier of treatment) show signal; feature_002, feature_016, feature_028 are inert for pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each rare binary: ttest_ind on pfs; trt:feat interaction p
analyses.result_summary: feature_002 (0.5% prev): main diff +0.04 (p=0.79); trt-interaction p=0.20. feature_016 (5%): main diff -0.02 (p=0.68); trt-interaction p=0.85. feature_028 (3%): main diff -0.06 (p=0.36); trt-interaction p=0.46. feature_011 and feature_022 (already characterized): both show signal as discussed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (continuous, mean 3.8) is independently positively associated with pfs_months (consistent with a beneficial baseline labor as e.g. albumin); feature_025 (continuous, mean 3.8, many zeros) is independently negatively associated (consistent with a harmful exposure).

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ all features) — adjusted coefficients on feature_019 and feature_025
analyses.result_summary: feature_019: adjusted slope=+0.469 mo per unit, p<1e-300. feature_025: adjusted slope=-0.076 mo per unit, p<1e-300. Both independent of treatment. Neither significantly modifies feature_027 effect (p>0.10).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: feature_027 confers a ~+5 month gain in pfs_months specifically and only in patients defined by ALL FOUR predicates (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5). Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null. Each of the four predicates is independently necessary; no other feature further refines the responder subgroup.

analyses.code: Confirm subgroup definition: ttest within and outside subgroup; verify each predicate's necessity (h18); verify no further refinement (h23)
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=9338, ~18.7% of cohort): mean PFS on treatment = 9.10 mo, off treatment = 4.09 mo; treatment effect = +5.01 mo (95% bootstrap CI [4.91, 5.12], p<1e-300). Complement (n=40662): treatment effect = +0.04 mo (p=0.20). Each of four predicates is independently necessary: removing any one drops the effect to <0.12 mo (n.s.). No additional features (feature_001, feature_014, feature_011, feature_015) further modify the effect within the subgroup. This four-condition rule fully characterizes the responder population.
